method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/cchr (Akamai + nginx + AWS ALB + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform + Dynatrace + Akamai mPulse) and read the 'Report Discrimination' intake form (/about/report-discrimination.page), identified as a native server-rendered Livesite HTML form with no machine-readable contract — its category choices map one-to-one to the Inquiries Received dataset columns. Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Commission on Human Rights (CCHR)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 3 assets with column schemas.

meta.platform: NYC.gov shared 'Livesite' platform (Akamai edge, nginx, AWS ALB, Dynatrace + mPulse RUM) — no CCHR-specific application platform and, unlike NYCHA, no vendor CRM.
meta.netnew: A typed DiscriminationComplaint intake API (report discrimination) — replacing the untyped Report Discrimination HTML form.
meta.tagline: CCHR's core transaction — reporting discrimination — is an untyped web form with no API, backed by only three aggregate datasets and a Human Rights Law published as prose and PDF.
meta.headline: The fix is to give the discrimination complaint, the protected classes, and the legal guidance a structured, agent-native contract.

scorecard_notes.platform: Modern managed NYC.gov 'Livesite' chassis (Akamai/nginx/AWS ALB/Dynatrace) but shared and content-only.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No machine-readable site/content API; the intake is an HTML form, not an application.
scorecard_notes.openData: Only 3 thin, aggregate operational datasets on Socrata SODA; no law, guidance, or case data.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Core entities (complaints, cases, protected classes, guidance) exist as none-structured — form fields, prose, and PDF.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: Report Discrimination is an untyped HTML form posting to an opaque backend; no write API.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface of any kind; nothing an agent can call.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Runs on the city's own platform with no vendor CRM lock-in; but the case backend is opaque and the intake is untyped.

headline_findings: CCHR is the thinnest domain yet: NYC Open Data carries only 3 CCHR datasets — Inquiries Received, Office of Mediation cases, and Pre-Complaint Resolutions — and all three are aggregate operational counts, not case data.

The core citizen transaction, reporting discrimination, is a plain server-rendered HTML form on the shared NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform that posts into an opaque Law Enforcement Bureau backend — no API, no OpenAPI, no JSON, no documented POST schema.
The NYC Human Rights Law itself — protected classes and legal enforcement guidance — is published only as prose and PDF; 'is X protected in housing?' has no machine-readable answer.

Individual complaint/respondent/case data is never published (privacy by design); the intake form's typed structure is discarded and re-surfaces only as an annual tally whose columns match the form's own category choices.
Net-new write surface: report discrimination (DiscriminationComplaint) — typing the untyped intake form. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: Only 3 CCHR datasets (395v-hkhg, tmha-56pf, 6ayi-8khd); SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one open API — aggregate operational counts only.
The core transaction. Server-rendered HTML <form> (name, category, respondent, incident date, description, up to 3 attachments). No OpenAPI/JSON; posts to an opaque Law Enforcement Bureau backend.
Shared NYC.gov mail/contact-form validation service referenced by the page — not a CCHR complaint API.
The law, legal library, complaint process, trainings/workshops — content only. Akamai edge, nginx, AWS ALB, Dynatrace + mPulse RUM. No content API.

fruit.name: Report Discrimination
fruit.notes: The single most important resident transaction — report discrimination and (optionally) file a complaint. A native NYC.gov 'Livesite' HTML form with typed-looking fields (your name/pronouns/address/email/phone; category of discrimination; respondent name/location/phone; date of most recent incident; prior-complaint Yes/No; free-text problem; up to 3 attachments; how you heard about the Commission) but NO API and NO Open Data twin. Its data is discarded as structure and re-surfaces only as annual inquiry counts. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

fruit.name: Protected classes (NYC Human Rights Law)
fruit.notes: The categories protected under Title 8 (age, race, gender identity, disability, source of income, etc.) and the protected areas (employment, housing, public accommodations, lending, discriminatory harassment, bias-based profiling). Published only as prose; no list, no API, no dataset.

fruit.name: Legal enforcement guidance & publications
fruit.notes: CCHR legal enforcement guidance, fact sheets, and Legal Library publications (gender identity/expression, race/hairstyle, source of income, Fair Chance Act, disability). Published only as HTML pages and PDFs; no catalog API and no Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Trainings, workshops & outreach
fruit.notes: Know-your-rights workshops, the Stop Sexual Harassment Act training, TGNC/Fair Chance campaigns, and Community Relations Bureau outreach — the Commission's education mission. HTML pages only; no events API, no dataset. Also the home of bias-testing/education programs.

fruit.name: Inquiries received
fruit.notes: AGGREGATE annual counts of inquiries about discrimination by protected area (Employment, Housing, Public Accommodations, Lending Practices, Discriminatory Harassment, Bias-Based Profiling) with a Grand Total. The columns match the intake form's category choices — internal structure exists but only tallies are published.

fruit.name: Mediation & pre-complaint resolutions
opendata_match: Office of Mediation and Conflict Resolution Cases tmha-56pf (3c)
fruit.notes: Two parallel AGGREGATE monthly-count datasets — settlements closed by the Office of Mediation, and pre-complaint resolutions. No individual case is published.
